mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or signal loses predictive power during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and declining federal funds rates amplifies consumer spending power and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over any 3-year rolling window, or joint signal underperforms single-proxy baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects robust manufacturing output and supply chain stability, which supports near-term equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during periods of supply chain disruption or when inflation exceeds 4.0%, or positive return frequency drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market and rising aggregate income, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive return frequency drops below 50% when PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k per month over a 2-year sample, or Rank IC < 0.02 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 310.0 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL crosses the 310.0 threshold, or signal fails to predict direction during disinflationary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and declines in the unemployment rate signal synchronized strength in construction and labor markets, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model (Sharpe ratio < 0.5) over a 3-year out-of-sample period, or interaction effect vanishes when housing inventory rises.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms sustained economic expansion, supporting corporate earnings growth and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative when GDP growth exceeds 3.0% due to overheating fears, or Rank IC < 0.02 in early-cycle regimes over any 3-year window.
